title: 2026 is the Year of the ‘Agentic UI’ and How to Build One Using Gemini 3.0, Goodbye Chatbots!
author: Vishal Shrestha
text:
Member-only story
2026 is the Year of the ‘Agentic UI’ and How to Build One Using Gemini 3.0, Goodbye Chatbots!
You’re probably tired of hearing about “AI Agents”, and I get it. The term has been beaten to death by every “AI influencer” and Series A startup pitch deck in the last eighteen months.
But here’s the thing: most people are still building chatbots and calling them agents. They’re slapping a text box on a webpage, streaming some tokens, and patting themselves on the back.
I’ve been developing since the early 2010s, and if there’s one thing I’ve learned about software, it’s that users hate friction. And the text box? It’s good at answering your queries, but when it comes to actual execution, you are the one who has to put it all together and make it happen. Talk about friction, right?
We taught users to talk to machines. We did not teach machines to act like competent collaborators.
All that has changed.
2026 isn’t about better text generation. It’s about the Agentic UI.
Interfaces that don’t just talk, but morph. Interfaces that understand intent and execute the necessary tools right in front of your eyes.
Let’s tear down the chatbot and build something that actually feels like the future using Google’s freshly minted Gemini 3.0.
